choices.text: 6 Keplerian elements: a (size), e (shape), i (tilt), Ω (RAAN), ω (perigee), ν (current position). Kepler's laws: ellipses around central body, equal areas swept in equal times, T² ∝ a³.
Vis-viva equation: v² = GM(2/r - 1/a). Speed varies in elliptical orbit (faster at perigee, slower at apogee). Total energy E = -GM/(2a) constant — determined by a alone.
